hypothesis: Incorporating temperature-dependent molecular conformational dynamics into foundation model pre-training—by augmenting SMILES corpora with thermally-sampled 3D conformer ensembles paired with their corresponding temperatures—will enable a single unified model to predict both intrinsic molecular properties and extrinsic formulation performance metrics (such as ionic conductivity across temperature ranges) with lower out-of-distribution error than current SMILES-only foundation models, because the thermal conformational vocabulary will encode the entropy-driven solvation shell reorganization that governs macroscale electrolyte behavior but is absent from static structural representations.
